Off Calendar Sep-23-2019 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been continued from September 9, 2019, for trial/no appearance by defendant(s).
Judge: Gail Dekreon; Clerk: J. Bertelsen; Reported by Vicki Gross, CSR#5525, vgross@sftc.org, 415-551-3730 (514)

Plaintiff Lu Vaccaro appearing in pro per.
No appearance on behalf of or by Defendant Paul Dennison Candelori.
Court grants Plaintiff's request to amend the complaint to add a cause of action of conversion.
After evidence and review of declarations, Court ordered the following Judgment entered in favor of Plaintiff Lu Vaccaro and against Defendant Paul Dennison Candelori as follows:
Principal $90,718.50
Pre-Judgment Interest $9979.35
Attorney's Fees $6907.31
Costs $510
Other: punitives $81,646.65
Total Judgment in the amount of $189,761.81
Judgment signed in open court.

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Julien Swanson of the law offices of Austin Law Group (415-282-4511) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Michael J. Guglielmino of the law offices of M.J. Guglielmino (415-221-7174) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

Court confers with counsel for both sides.

PLAINTIFF LU VACCARO'S Stipulated Motion Seeking Leave To Hear Discovery Motion And Complete Discovery Closer To Trial Date is GRANTED. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Notice Of Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Julien Swanson of the law offices of Austin Law Group (415-282-4511) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Michael J. Guglielmino of the law offices of M.J. Guglielmino (415-221-7174) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides.

PLAINTIFF LU VACCARO'S Motion To Compel Discovery, Deem Matters Admitted, and for Monetary Sanctions, filed June 28, 2017 is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The motion to compel is granted as to verified responses. The request for admissions to be deemed admitted and the request for monetary sanctions are denied. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.
